title: General Clothing Provision * Homelessness Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > General Clothing Provision * Homelessness Issues 8 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter AIDS/HIV At Risk Youth Homeless Families Homeless Men Homeless Youth Homelessness Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF ASTORIA RESCUE MISSION COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Serves three community meals including breakfast, lunch, and dinner. Operates a clothing closet, showers, and a laundry facility allowing one load per week. Accepts donations of hygiene products such as shampoo, conditioner, body wash, toilet paper, and toothpaste. 62 W Bond Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Breakfast: 7 days per week 7am Lunch: 7 days per week noon Dinner: 7 days per week 5:30pm Showers: 7 days per week 3:30pm-5pm Laundry: 7 days per week 3:30pm-5pm Clothing Closet: 7 days per week 11am-5pm Email (503) 325-6243 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BROOKINGS CORE RESPONSE PEER HOUSE NAVIGATION CENTER PEER HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Navigation center offers clothing, toiletries, restrooms, light snacks, and beverages, bus passes (10 per month, by availability), internet access, device charging station, a community phone, and a temporary mailing address. Also provides a secure location for medication storage, including refrigeration and Narcan/Naloxone on site. 16389 Highway 101 S Brookings, OR 97415 Eligibility: Medication storage: Unsheltered individuals All other services: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-3pm Email (541) 251-0825 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HYGIENE4ALL HYGIENE4ALL HUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a community hygiene, sanitation, and safety hub. Provides showers, bedding in exchange for soiled, a clothing swap, menstruation and moontime hygiene supplies, first-aid kits, bathrooms, legal disposal for household trash and needles, foot, wound, and mouth care, and a warm outdoor space with coffee and tea. Also offers paid work and opportunities for advocacy. 824 SE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Homeless individuals and families Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 3pm-8:30pm Open regardless of weather. Last shower 45 minutes before close. Search for Hygiene4all Hub on Google Maps for unanticipated holiday or other closures. Email (971) 236-2524 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEBOAT SERVICES DAYTIME NAVIGATION SHELTER LIFEBOAT DAY SHELTER AND NAVIGATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a daytime drop-in center with access to socks, laundry facilities, Wi-Fi, computer and telephone access, fax, coffee, snacks, a clothing closet, assistance in navigating social services, and specialized information and referral. Camping gear may be available, depending on donations. 1076 Franklin Avenue Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Saturday/Sunday 6am-6pm, Thursday/Friday 8am-6pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST HUMAN SERVICES HOMELESS OUTREACH & ADVOCACY PROJECT HOMELESS OUTREACH AND ADVOCACY PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center. Services include information and referral, laundry, clothing, showers, lunch, mailbox access, mental health support groups, and peer support services. Also connects clients with medical services at West Salem Medical Clinic. 694 Church Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Individuals who are homeless or at risk of becoming homeless Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-1pm and 2pm-4pm Appointments: Monday-Friday 2pm-4pm Email (503) 588-5827 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPERATION NIGHTWATCH DOWNTOWN HOSPITALITY CENTER HOSPITALITY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a safe place where guests can drop in and socialize, play games, and read books. The center provides water, coffee, a hot meal, blankets, socks, and mental health services. 1432 SW 13th Avenue Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: People experiencing housing insecurity Hours: Thursday-Saturday 6:30pm-10pm Email (503) 220-0438 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY EUGENE SERVICE STATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a day center. Services include laundry facilities, showers, meals, phone access, voicemail services, clothing, blankets, personal care supplies, job search assistance, and information and referral to other services. Visitors can use indoor, climate-controlled seating and outdoor, shaded picnic tables. 450 Highway 99 N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Low-income and homeless adults without minor children Hours: 7 days per week 8:30am-5pm Breakfast: 7 days per week 8:30am Lunch: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday/Saturday/Sunday noon, Wednesday 11am Showers and laundry: 7 days per week 8:30am-noon and 2pm-5pm Email (541) 689-6747 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITION PROJECTS RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance in accessing services and agencies to help people transition out of homelessness into stable housing. The center offers showers, laundry, haircuts, clothing, lockers, and mailing address service. 650 NW Irving Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Age 18 and older experiencing homelessness Hours: Resource Center: Monday-Friday 8:30am-7pm, Saturday/Sunday/all holidays: 8:30am-3pm. All service intakes end 15 minutes before close. 1st Thursday and 3rd Tuesday of the month the Resource Center closes at noon. Restroom and showers: Monday-Friday 8:30am-7pm, Saturday/Sunday/all holidays: 8:30am-3pm Clothing: Monday/Friday 10am-11am, Wednesday 1pm-2pm Haircuts: Wednesday-Friday, times vary Mailroom: Monday-Friday 8:30am-10am and 12:45pm-3:30pm, Saturday 1pm-3pm (503) 280-4700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ASTORIA RESCUE MISSION COMMUNITY SERVICES BROOKINGS CORE RESPONSE PEER HOUSE HYGIENE4ALL HYGIENE4ALL HUB LIFEBOAT SERVICES LIFEBOAT DAY SHELTER AND NAVIGATION CENTER NORTHWEST HUMAN SERVICES HOMELESS OUTREACH AND ADVOCACY PROJECT OPERATION NIGHTWATCH HOSPITALITY CENTER ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY EUGENE SERVICE STATION TRANSITION PROJECTS RESOURCE CENTER
